d: Bottom navigation: 3-5 primary destinations fixed at screen bottom. iOS tab bar = 49pt height + 34pt safe area inset. Android bottom nav = 80dp height. ALWAYS show text labels beneath icons. Active state: filled icon + brand color. Inactive: outlined icon + neutral color. Best pattern for top-level destinations. Avoid >5 items (use hamburger for overflow). Badge indicators for notifications (red dot or count). Haptic feedback on tap recommended.

Horizontal scrollable tabs for filtering content within a section. Height: 48dp/pt. Use for content categories, time periods, data views. Indicator line (2-3dp) under active tab. Scrollable when >4 tabs. Fixed when <=4 tabs (equal width distribution). Tab label: single line, max ~12 characters. Active tab should be visually distinct (bold text + indicator + color). Swipe between tab content horizontally.

Hidden navigation behind 3-line menu icon (hamburger). Drawer slides in from left edge (LTR) or right (RTL). Width: 256-320dp, never full screen width (leave ~56dp visible). Pros: saves space for many nav items. Cons: low discoverability, extra tap to access. Best for: secondary navigation, settings, account, less-used features. RULE: Avoid as primary navigation on mobile — bottom nav has higher engagement. Can combine: bottom nav for primary + drawer for secondary.

Hierarchical push/pop navigation pattern. New screens push onto stack from right (iOS) or bottom/right (Android). Back button (top-left iOS, system back Android) pops to previous screen. Maintain scroll position when returning. Support deep linking: any screen should be reachable via URL. Breadcrumb trail optional for complex hierarchies. Transition animation: 300-350ms slide. Preserve user input state when navigating back. Limit stack depth to avoid user confusion (5-7 levels max practical).

FAB: 56dp circular button for THE single primary action on a screen. Position: bottom-right with 16-24dp margin from edges. ONE per screen maximum. Use for: compose, add, create actions. Don't use for minor/destructive actions. Can expand to speed dial showing 3-6 related actions on long press or tap. Mini FAB: 40dp for secondary screens. Extended FAB: pill shape with icon+label for clearer intent. Hide on scroll down, show on scroll up. Elevation: 6dp default, 12dp pressed.

Toolbar appears when content is selected (long press to enter selection mode). Replaces app bar temporarily with action icons: delete, share, move, archive, etc. Shows selection count. Provide select-all option. Dismiss: back button or tap outside selection. iOS: toolbar at bottom during edit mode. Android: contextual app bar at top (replaces regular app bar). Actions should be relevant to selected content type. Destructive actions require confirmation.

Easy reach green zone: bottom center third of screen. This is the natural resting position of the thumb. Place primary actions, main CTAs, and frequently used controls here. Bottom navigation, FABs, and primary buttons all belong in this zone. For right-handed users (majority): bottom-right is easiest. For left-handed: bottom-left. Design for both by centering critical actions. On larger phones (>6 inch), this zone shrinks proportionally.

t: Thumb Zone - Stretch and Hard Reach
d: Stretch zone (yellow): sides and middle of screen — place secondary content, scrollable lists, readable text here. Hard reach zone (red): top corners — place infrequent actions here (settings gear, search icon, profile avatar, overflow menu). RULE: Most important interactions within thumb reach (bottom 60% of screen). One-handed use: keep primary actions within 70mm arc from bottom corner. Avoid actions requiring stretch to opposite top corner. Use bottom sheets instead of top-positioned menus to bring actions into thumb zone.

Tap: the primary interaction gesture. Use for: select items, activate buttons, toggle switches, navigate links, dismiss dialogs. Response: immediate visual feedback (<100ms). Double-tap: secondary gesture for zoom in/out (maps, images, documents) or like/favorite (social media pattern, e.g., Instagram). Double-tap timing window: 300ms between taps. RULE: Never make double-tap the ONLY way to perform an action — always provide single-tap alternative.

Long press (press-and-hold): 500ms threshold to trigger. Use for: reveal context menu, enter selection/edit mode, show link/image preview (iOS peek), drag-to-reorder. Provide haptic feedback on activation. Visual feedback: item subtly scales up or highlights during press. RULE: Long press has low discoverability — never make it the only way to access critical functionality. Always provide visible alternative (overflow menu icon, edit button). iOS: context menu with blur background. Android: popup menu or bottom sheet.

Swipe horizontal: navigate between pages/tabs (left/right), dismiss cards, reveal hidden actions. Swipe-to-reveal: partially swipe list item to show action buttons (delete, archive, pin). iOS: swipe left for destructive (red delete), swipe right for positive actions. Swipe threshold: 30-40% of item width to commit action, else snap back. Full swipe: immediately executes action. Partial swipe: reveals buttons. Provide visual cues: peeking colored background or icon. RULE: Always offer undo for destructive swipe actions (snackbar with undo).

Swipe vertical: primary scrolling gesture. Pull-to-refresh: pull content down past threshold (~60-80pt) to trigger refresh, spinner appears at top. Release to execute refresh. Swipe down on bottom sheets/modals to dismiss (velocity-based: fast swipe = dismiss, slow = elastic). Swipe up on bottom sheet to expand to full screen. Scroll momentum: content continues scrolling with deceleration after finger lifts. Rubber-band effect at scroll boundaries (iOS). Edge glow at boundaries (Android).

Pinch: two-finger gesture for zoom in/out on maps, images, documents, web content. Pinch center point = zoom anchor. Double-tap to reset zoom level. Edge swipe (system-level): Android left/right edge swipe = system back gesture. iOS left edge swipe = navigate back in stack. RULE: Never override system edge gestures — users expect consistent system behavior. Reserve 20dp from screen edges for system gestures (Android). RULE: Always provide visible button alternatives for any gesture-based action for accessibility (switch control, motor impairment users).

Gestures are invisible by default — users must discover them. Techniques: show peeking content at screen edge (swipeable hint), display handle bars on bottom sheets (small gray pill), use coach marks on first use, animate a gentle nudge/bounce to suggest swipe direction. Tooltip overlays for new gesture features. RULE: Every gesture must have a visible button alternative. RULE: First-time use: consider showing a brief animation demonstrating the gesture. RULE: Don't rely on gestures alone for critical actions — discoverability is ~40-60% lower than visible buttons.

WCAG 2.1 AA color contrast requirements. Normal text (<18pt or <14pt bold): minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio against background. Large text (>=18pt or >=14pt bold): minimum 3:1 contrast ratio. UI components and graphical objects: minimum 3:1 against adjacent colors. RULE: Never use color alone to convey information (error states, status, required fields) — always add icon, text label, or pattern. Test with contrast checker tools. Common fails: light gray text on white, low-contrast placeholder text. Placeholder text should meet 4.5:1 or use label instead.

Minimum touch target sizes: iOS = 44x44pt, Android = 48x48dp. These are MINIMUM sizes — larger is better for primary actions (56dp+ for FAB). Spacing between adjacent touch targets: minimum 8dp/pt to prevent mis-taps. Visual element can be smaller than touch target (e.g., 24dp icon with 48dp tap area using padding). RULE: Text links in body text need sufficient line height and padding to meet target size. RULE: Close/dismiss buttons in corners must still meet minimum size. RULE: Test with users who have motor impairments.

All interactive elements need accessible labels (accessibilityLabel on iOS, contentDescription on Android). Images: meaningful images need descriptive alt text; decorative images marked as hidden from accessibility tree. Focus order: logical reading order (top-to-bottom, left-to-right for LTR). Focus indicators: visible ring (2dp+ width) with sufficient contrast for keyboard/switch navigation. Heading hierarchy: use proper H1-H6 for screen reader navigation (allows jump-to-heading). RULE: Test with VoiceOver (iOS) and TalkBack (Android). RULE: Announce dynamic content changes (loading complete, error appeared) to screen readers.

Text scaling: support Dynamic Type (iOS) and font scaling (Android) up to 200%. Layout must reflow — text should never be clipped or overlap at maximum size. Test with largest accessibility text setting. Fixed-size text only for elements that truly cannot scale (icons, badges). Motion: respect Reduce Motion preference (iOS: UIAccessibility.isReduceMotionEnabled, Android: Settings > Accessibility). Replace animations with instant state changes or subtle fades. Parallax, auto-playing videos, and bouncy transitions should all be disabled when Reduce Motion is on.

Skeleton screens: show layout structure with gray placeholder shapes that match upcoming content dimensions. Animated shimmer effect (left-to-right gradient sweep) indicates loading in progress. Preferred over spinners for content-heavy screens (feeds, lists, profiles). Benefits: reduces perceived loading time by ~30%, prevents layout shift when content loads. Structure: match actual content layout — rectangle for images, thin bars for text lines, circles for avatars. RULE: Skeleton shapes should closely match real content proportions. RULE: Use skeleton for initial page loads, not subsequent interactions.

t: Spinners, Progress Bars, and Optimistic UI
d: Spinner: use for short indeterminate actions (<3 seconds). Center in content area or inline within button (button becomes disabled with spinner). Don't use spinner alone on empty screen — show app shell + skeleton. Progress bar: use for determinate operations (file upload, download, sync). Show percentage or fraction (3/10 items). Pull-to-refresh: standard refresh pattern — pull content down, spinner at top. Optimistic UI: show result immediately, sync in background, rollback with error toast on failure. RULE: <1s = instant feel (no indicator), 1-3s = spinner acceptable, >3s = show progress indicator with estimate.

t: Lazy Loading and Content Loading Strategy
d: Lazy loading: load content as user scrolls toward it. Trigger load when user is ~2-3 screens away from end of loaded content. Show skeleton placeholders for upcoming items during load. Images: load thumbnail/blur first, then full resolution (progressive loading). Infinite scroll vs pagination: infinite scroll for feeds/social, pagination for search results/catalogs. Infinite scroll: show loading indicator at bottom during fetch. RULE: Always show a loading indicator — never leave user wondering if more content exists. RULE: Provide end-of-content indicator when all items are loaded.

t: Empty States: First Use, No Results, Error
d: First-use empty state: welcome message + illustration + clear CTA to get started (Add first item, Create first project). Guide the user — show value proposition. No results (search/filter): acknowledge the query (No results for X), suggest alternatives (Try different keywords), provide clear-filters button, show popular/trending items. Error empty state: explain what went wrong in plain language + retry button + help/support link. RULE: Empty states are opportunities to guide users, not dead ends. RULE: Always include at least one action (CTA button) in every empty state.

t: Empty States: Deleted, Cleared, and Offline
d: Deleted/cleared state: confirm the action was completed. Offer undo via snackbar/toast at bottom of screen with undo button (show for 5-10 seconds before auto-dismiss). After undo window closes, action is permanent. Offline empty state: explain no internet connection clearly, show cached/previously loaded content if available, provide retry/refresh button, show connection status indicator. RULE: App should degrade gracefully — show cached data with offline badge rather than blocking the entire UI. RULE: Differentiate between empty (no items exist) and offline (items exist but can't be fetched).

Inline validation: validate field when it loses focus (on blur), not while typing (on change). Show error immediately: red/error-color border (2dp) + error icon + descriptive error text below field (12-14sp). Clear error state when user starts correcting input. Success state: green border + checkmark for confirmed valid input (optional). RULE: Error messages should say what's wrong AND how to fix it (Password must be at least 8 characters not Invalid password). RULE: Don't validate empty required fields until form submission attempt. RULE: Never use red color alone for errors — add icon + text for colorblind users.

t: Toast, Snackbar, Banner, and Full-Screen Errors
d: Toast/snackbar: brief non-blocking message at bottom of screen for minor errors or confirmations. Auto-dismiss in 4-10 seconds. Include action button (Retry, Undo). Single line preferred, max 2 lines. Banner/alert: persistent message at top of screen for important ongoing issues (no network, account problem). Include dismiss or action. Does not auto-dismiss. Full-screen error: for critical failures (server down, no network + no cache). Show illustration + plain-language message + retry button + help link. RULE: Show errors close to source: inline > toast > banner > full-screen (escalation hierarchy). RULE: Error messages: what happened + how to fix (not technical codes).

Progressive disclosure: teach features contextually as user encounters them naturally, not all upfront. First encounter with a feature triggers brief explanation. Coach marks: translucent overlay with tooltip pointing to specific UI element. Brief explanation text (1-2 sentences max). Sequential: max 3-5 steps per flow. Include Skip button and step indicator (2/5). Highlight target element, dim everything else. RULE: Don't block core usage — let users dismiss and explore freely. RULE: Coach marks should only appear once (track first-run state). RULE: Critical features can re-show coach marks if never used after 3 sessions.

Walkthrough carousel: 3-5 full-screen slides showing key features and value proposition. Each slide: illustration/screenshot + headline + brief description (2 lines max). Include Skip button on every slide. Final slide: primary CTA (Get Started, Sign Up). Dot indicator showing progress. Permission priming: always explain WHY before triggering the system permission dialog. Show a custom pre-permission screen: benefit to user + what you'll use it for + Continue button (triggers real dialog) + Not Now option. RULE: Show value before asking for signup or payment. RULE: 50-60% higher permission grant rate with priming screens vs direct system prompt.

t: Mobile Form Layout and Input Design
d: One column layout: always single column for all form fields on mobile phones. Never place fields side-by-side on phone (exception: short related fields like city/state on tablet). Input field height: 48-56dp/pt with 16dp/pt horizontal padding. Label position: above the input field (not inside as placeholder-only — floating labels are acceptable as they move above on focus). Group related fields with section headers. Spacing between fields: 16-24dp. RULE: Minimize required fields — only ask what is essential. Progressive disclosure for optional fields (Show more / Advanced).

Match keyboard to input type: email keyboard for email fields (@ and .com visible), number pad for phone/OTP, URL keyboard for web addresses, decimal pad for currency. Auto-advance: automatically move focus to next field when current field is complete (OTP code digits). Return key types: Next for form field progression, Done for last field, Search for search input, Go for URL input. Submit button: full-width on mobile, descriptive label (Create Account not Submit). RULE: Support OS autofill — use correct HTML input types (email, tel, password) or native equivalents. RULE: Show/hide password toggle (eye icon) on all password fields.

Keyboard height: ~260-300pt (iOS), ~250dp (Android) — varies by device and keyboard app. RULE: Active input field MUST remain visible above keyboard — scroll content up automatically when keyboard appears. RULE: Don't cover the submit/action button with keyboard — scroll or reposition to keep it accessible. RULE: Dismiss keyboard: tap outside input area (add tap gesture to background), or provide Done/Dismiss button in toolbar above keyboard (iOS keyboard accessory view). RULE: Use correct return key type for context. Account for keyboard height in scroll padding (add bottom inset equal to keyboard height).

Respect system preference: follow OS dark/light mode setting by default (auto-switch). Provide manual toggle in app settings to override. Dark surfaces: use dark gray (#121212 for base, #1E1E1E for elevated cards, #2C2C2C for highest elevation) NOT pure black (#000000) — pure black causes excessive contrast and OLED smearing. Elevation in dark mode: lighter surface color = higher elevation (inverse of light mode shadow). White text hierarchy using opacity: primary text 87% opacity, secondary 60%, disabled 38%. RULE: Test both modes — don't treat dark mode as afterthought.

t: Dark Mode: Colors, Images, and Assets
d: Colored elements in dark mode: desaturate brand colors slightly (reduce saturation 10-20%) to reduce visual vibration against dark backgrounds. Use lighter tints of brand colors for buttons and accents. Reduce image brightness in dark mode: apply 10-15% dark overlay on images/photos to match ambient darkness. Illustrations: provide separate dark mode variants or auto-adjust with CSS/native filters. Icons: ensure sufficient contrast on dark surfaces (may need lighter stroke/fill variants). RULE: Never invert colors naively — design intentionally for dark mode. RULE: Shadows are invisible on dark surfaces — use lighter borders or elevated surface colors instead.

Cache-first strategy: show cached/stored content immediately on launch, then fetch updates in background. Update UI seamlessly when fresh data arrives (avoid jarring full-page reloads). Offline indicator: subtle banner or status bar icon showing offline status — don't block entire UI. Queue user actions: save edits, posts, likes, and other write operations to a local queue. Sync queue automatically when connection is restored. Show pending sync indicator on queued items. RULE: App should always show SOMETHING — never a blank screen. RULE: Offline-capable features should work silently; online-only features should explain gracefully with retry option.

Perceived performance techniques: show content progressively (header first, then body, then images). Use skeleton screens during data fetch. Animate transitions between states (300ms ease) to mask loading. Pre-fetch likely next screens (e.g., pre-load detail page when user hovers/pauses on list item). Image optimization: lazy load offscreen images, serve responsive sizes (1x/2x/3x), use modern formats (WebP/AVIF), show blurred thumbnail placeholder (LQIP) then crossfade to full resolution. RULE: Time to interactive should be <3 seconds on 3G. RULE: First contentful paint <1.5 seconds.

Accessibility testing checklist for mobile UX: 1) Color contrast meets 4.5:1 (normal text) and 3:1 (large text, UI components). 2) All touch targets >= 44pt (iOS) / 48dp (Android). 3) Screen reader announces all elements correctly (test VoiceOver + TalkBack). 4) Focus order is logical. 5) Text scales to 200% without content loss. 6) Animations respect Reduce Motion. 7) No information conveyed by color alone. 8) Error messages are descriptive and announced. 9) All gestures have button alternatives. 10) Heading hierarchy (H1-H6) is correct. 11) Dynamic content changes are announced. 12) Keyboard/switch navigation works for all flows. RULE: Test with real assistive technologies, not just automated tools.
